commit.message:
feat(overlayfs): add Prometheus metrics instrumentation (#125)

* feat(overlayfs): add Prometheus metrics instrumentation

Add optional Prometheus metrics to the overlay filesystem via the
WithMetrics(reg) functional option on NewOverlayFS. When not enabled,
all instrumentation is a no-op with zero overhead.

Metrics implemented per §7.2 of the overlay FS design doc:
- blogflow_overlay_resolve_duration_seconds (Histogram by op)
- blogflow_overlay_layer_hit_total (Counter by layer name)
- blogflow_overlay_miss_total (Counter)
- blogflow_overlay_negcache_hit_total (Counter)
- blogflow_overlay_negcache_size (Gauge)
- blogflow_overlay_path_rejected_total (Counter by reason)

Tests cover: metric registration, counter increments on layer hits,
histogram duration recording, path rejection classification, negative
cache metrics, and no-panic when metrics are disabled.

Fixes #9

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(overlayfs): replace MustRegister with Register + error propagation

- Change newOverlayMetrics to return (*overlayMetrics, error) using
  individual reg.Register() calls instead of MustRegister (prevents
  panic on duplicate registration)
- Change Option type to func(*OverlayFS) error so options can surface
  registration failures
- Change NewOverlayFS to return (*OverlayFS, error) to propagate
  option errors to callers
- Add explicit parentheses in classifyInvalidPath for clarity
- Add TestMetrics_DuplicateRegistrationReturnsError test
- Update all callers and tests for new signatures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(overlayfs): rollback partial metric registration and sync gauge in ReplaceLayer

- newOverlayMetrics now unregisters previously-registered collectors if a
  mid-loop registration fails, preventing orphaned metrics in the registry.
- ReplaceLayer now updates negCacheSize gauge after clearing neg-cache entries,
  keeping the gauge consistent with InvalidateLayer/InvalidateAll.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(scanner): add best-effort error collection mode (#124)

* feat(scanner): add best-effort error collection mode

Add WithBestEffort() ScanOption for the content scanner that collects
parse errors, duplicate slugs, and slug conflicts instead of aborting.

- ScanOption type with functional options pattern (backward compatible)
- In best-effort mode: partial Index returned with nil error
- Call Index.Errors() to retrieve collected errors
- Default strict mode behavior unchanged (fail-fast)
- 10 new tests covering best-effort and strict mode parity

Fixes #19

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(scanner): harden best-effort mode

- Return defensive copy from Errors() to prevent callers mutating internal state
- Collect non-ErrNotExist WalkDir traversal errors in best-effort mode
  instead of aborting the scan (e.g. permission denied on subdirectory)
- Log collected_errors count at scan completion for observability

commit.message:
feat(theme): add context.Context to Render methods (#123)

* feat(theme): add context.Context to Render methods

Add context.Context as the first parameter to Engine.Render and
Engine.RenderToString. Before template execution, the context is
checked for cancellation so that long-running renders can be
aborted by callers (e.g. on client disconnect).

All callers updated:
- handlers.renderTemplate now passes r.Context()
- All theme tests pass context.Background()
- New tests verify cancelled-context returns context.Canceled
  and valid-context renders succeed.

Fixes #21

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(handlers): downgrade context-cancelled render errors to Debug

When a client disconnects mid-render, r.Context() is cancelled and
engine.Render returns context.Canceled. Previously this logged at Error
level and attempted an HTTP 500 response to a closed connection.

Now renderTemplate checks r.Context().Err() first and silently returns
after a Debug log, keeping production error logs clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: tiered L2 classifier for code-generation prompts

Replace binary SIMPLE/COMPLEX classifier with a three-tier system:
- TEMPLATE: config files, type definitions, documentation
- SNIPPET: short single-function code, simple middleware
- COMPLEX: multi-file implementations, test suites, full endpoints

This allows L2 to deflect ~35% of code-generation benchmark prompts
that were previously all routed to L3 because the old classifier
explicitly listed 'code generation' as COMPLEX.

Changes:
- Add ClassifierMode enum (binary/tiered) to config
- Add CLASSIFY_TIERED_SYSTEM_PROMPT in slm_triage.rs
- Add answer_quality_ok() guard (rejects <10 char, uncertainty phrases)
- Add max_answer_tokens config field (default 2048)
- Add classify_deflectable() to SlmClient for proxy path
- Update proxy embedded + sidecar paths for tiered classification
- Tag fixture prompts with expected_classification field
- Add tests: TEMPLATE/SNIPPET short-circuit, binary backward compat,
  quality guard unit tests
- Default mode is 'tiered'; set classifier_mode=binary for old behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(overlayfs): add ContextOverlayFS with context support (#122)

* feat(overlayfs): add ContextOverlayFS with context support

Implement ContextOverlayFS wrapping *OverlayFS with context.Context on
all methods (Open, ReadFile, ReadDir, Stat, OpenFile) for:

- Context cancellation: ctx.Done() checked before each operation
- Security logging: WARN on path traversal with request_id, remote_addr
- Operation tracing: slog-based span-like logging (op, path, duration)

Unexported context key types (RequestIDKey, RemoteAddrKey) prevent
collisions. Nil logger is safe. No hard OTel dependency — actual
tracing can be wired later via context values.

Tests cover cancellation, security logging, all FS methods through
the wrapper, nil logger safety, and error propagation.

Fixes #5

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(overlayfs): nil-inner guard and Inner() accessor for ContextOverlayFS

- Panic in NewContextOverlayFS when inner is nil (programming error)
- Add Inner() method so stdlib consumers can access the underlying fs.FS
- Add tests for both

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(overlayfs): correct Open doc comment, add slog.Enabled guard

- Fix misleading comment on Open: said "before each layer attempt" but
  context is checked once before delegating to inner OverlayFS.
- Add slog.Enabled(ctx, LevelDebug) guard in logOperation to skip attr
  allocation when DEBUG is disabled in production.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Sort pages by weight then title (#121)

* Sort pages by weight then title

Add optional weight field (int, default 0) to page front matter.
Pages are now sorted deterministically: ascending by weight, then
alphabetically by title as a tiebreaker. Posts remain sorted by
date descending.

Fixes #20

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use case-insensitive title comparison in page sort tiebreaker

The title tiebreaker in the page weight sort used a case-sensitive
string comparison (ASCII ordering), which would sort "iOS Guide"
after "Zebra" since lowercase letters have higher codepoints than
uppercase. This is inconsistent with the codebase convention (e.g.
tags are lowercased) and produces surprising navigation order.

Use strings.ToLower on both sides of the comparison so the
tiebreaker is case-insensitive. Add a test exercising mixed-case
titles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: document sort order on Index.Pages field comment

Align Pages field comment with Posts field by documenting the
weight-ascending then title sort order introduced in this PR.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(theme): add Unicode/i18n support to urlize (#120)

* feat(theme): add Unicode/i18n support to urlize

Use NFKD normalization to decompose accented Latin characters and strip
combining marks so diacritics produce clean ASCII slugs (é→e, ü→u, ñ→n).
Non-Latin scripts (CJK, Cyrillic, Arabic, Hangul) are preserved as-is
for browsers to percent-encode. NFC recomposition restores decomposed
Hangul Jamo back into syllable blocks.

ASCII-only input behavior is unchanged (backward compatible).

Fixes #22

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(theme): handle non-decomposable Latin chars and add edge-case tests

- Add latinTranslit map for ß→ss, æ→ae, œ→oe, ø→o, đ→d, ł→l, þ→th, ð→d
  (NFKD does not decompose these characters)
- Add tests for Turkish İ, German ß, zero-width chars, soft hyphen, BOM
- Add tests for æ, œ, ø, ł, þ, ð transliteration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: round localStorage seconds to minute when above 4min

Stale localStorage could restore mid-minute value (e.g. 6477s)
causing seconds panels to show e.g. '57' at startup.
Above 4 minutes seconds are never displayed, so round up to
the nearest minute boundary on restore.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: A high-performance Minecraft Java Edition server rewritten in Rust. Targets Minecraft 26.1 — the first fully unobfuscated release.

commit.message:
fix: wire puller into PollStrategy after bootstrap (#118)

* fix: wire puller into PollStrategy after bootstrap

PollStrategy requires SetPuller() before Start(). The bootstrap
created a Puller for the initial clone but discarded it. main.go
now creates a Puller and calls SetPuller() on PollStrategy with
the configured repo URL, branch, and content path.

Fixes #117

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: reuse bootstrap Puller for poll wiring, eliminate duplication

bootstrapContent now returns the configured Puller, resolved branch, and
dest path. The poll-wiring block in main.go reuses the bootstrap Puller
instead of creating a second one with duplicate auth loading, sparse-dir
setup, and branch/dest defaulting.

This eliminates the DRY violation identified during review: both bootstrap
and poll wiring previously called LoadAuthFromEnv, NewPuller, and applied
identical defaults independently. A future change to Puller setup would
have required updates in two locations.

Bootstrap tests updated to verify returned Puller, branch, and dest values.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Convert loading overlay to 8px top bar; add persistent red-on-black error log (#213)

* Initial plan

* fix: replace full-screen loading overlay with 8px top bar; add red-on-black error log below it

- #loading-overlay: fixed top strip, 8px tall, full width, dark bg — no longer blocks the screen if it fails to hide on Android
- #progress-bar: full-width (was 240px centred), no border-radius
- Added #error-log: fixed below the bar (top: 8px), black bg, red monospace text, hidden until an exception fires, accumulates lines, scrollable up to 50vh
- addConsoleMessage(text, true) now appends to #error-log (visible while loading) instead of updating #console-log with an .error class
- Browser-capability warnings flow through console.error → #error-log (no longer appended into the loading-overlay DOM)
- Removed unused CSS: #console-log.error, #console-log:not(.error), .browser-warning

- docs/quickstart.md: fix health endpoint URL to /api/health, fix
  voucher API path and auth method (session cookie, not Bearer)
- docs/addon/config.md: fix audit_retention_days example (60, not 365)
- docs.py: remove non-existent /static/favicon.ico references
- audit_config.py: align prefix to /api/admin/audit (drop /v1/)
- security_headers.py: only set CSP if not already set by route handler
  (preserves guest portal unsafe-inline and Swagger/ReDoc inline needs)

commit.message:
Fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #26

- README: fix API docs URL to /admin/docs, update Python req to 3.13+
- docs/addon/config.md: align audit_retention_days with AuditConfig model
  (default 30, range 1-90)
- docs/quickstart.md: note local build option for Docker image
- audit_config: move from module-level global to app.state storage

commit.message:
fix: use dynamic API_BASE_URL in homeCompanies, companyDetails, companyList

Hardcoded 'http://localhost:3000' caused CSP violations and failed fetches
in production (Railway). All three files now use a ternary that returns
'http://localhost:3000/api/v1' on localhost and '/api/v1' (same-origin)
everywhere else — consistent with jobFilter.js, latestJobs.js, headerAuth.js.

Fixes: company grid on homepage, company list page, and company detail pages
in the Railway deployment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add optional Gaussian Process view with wallpaper group invariance (#12)

* Initial plan

* Add optional Gaussian Process view with wallpaper group invariance

- New: src/math/gaussianProcess.js — Fourier-based GP on 2D torus,
  symmetrized over point group by averaging across coset representatives
- New: src/math/__tests__/gaussianProcess.test.js — 12 tests covering
  dual lattice, coefficient drawing, periodicity, point-group extraction,
  heatmap generation, and symmetry invariance
- Modified: GroupVisualization.jsx — Renders GP heatmap as SVG image
- Modified: App.jsx — Adds Show GP toggle and New Draw button

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/jacksonloper/yetanotherwallpaperviewer/sessions/1262e67d-f11e-4375-a5a5-2cc4081fa5dc

* Fix GP heatmap not visible: prefer document.createElement('canvas') over OffscreenCanvas

OffscreenCanvas lacks synchronous toDataURL(), causing heatmapToDataURL
to return null in browsers. Now we use a regular canvas element (which
has toDataURL) when document is available, falling back to OffscreenCanvas
only in worker/non-DOM contexts.

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/jacksonloper/yetanotherwallpaperviewer/sessions/193575ad-a51b-4529-af6c-801d03b58ab2

* Add user controls for GP sigma, ell, and truncation n

Three new sliders appear when Show GP is checked:
- σ (amplitude): 0.1–3.0, default 1.0
- ℓ (length scale): 0.1–2.0, default 0.6
- n (truncation): 1–15, default 5

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/jacksonloper/yetanotherwallpaperviewer/sessions/a2db6ef6-dd27-42f8-9cb5-1d4ac5f85f46

* Remove sigma control, update ell default to 0.1 with range 0.02-0.2

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/jacksonloper/yetanotherwallpaperviewer/sessions/e65b1b74-f9d1-4b9f-9d0a-3beec69b8035

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
